01_initial_request.input_data: Pharmacogenomics (PGx) testing results and CPIC guidelines
01_initial_request.user_intent: To describe how PGx can be used to aid in more precise applications of pain pharmacotherapy based on CPIC guidelines
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Improved clinical response and safety profile of analgesics

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Patient presents with chronic pain and heterogeneity in pain management response is evident. [Gap] Specific genetic drivers of drug metabolism are unknown for this patient. [Decision] To achieve precise pain management, chose to use PGx panel to perform genotyping on patient DNA, expecting to obtain CYP2D6, CYP2C9, and CYP2C19 genotypes.

references.citation: Evans WE, McLeod HL (2003) Pharmacogenomics–drug disposition, drug targets, and side effects. N Engl J Med 348:538–549.
references.relevance: Defines PGx testing as a tool to support precise selection and dosing of pain medicines.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CYP2D6 PM status identified. [Gap] Standard opioid dosing (tramadol/codeine) relies on CYP2D6 activation. [Decision] To achieve safety and efficacy, chose to use CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and select opioid therapy to evaluate tramadol suitability, expecting to obtain recommendation to avoid use.

tool.name: CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and select opioid therapy

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recommendation to avoid codeine and tramadol use

references.citation: Crews KR, Monte AA, Huddart R et al (2021) Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium guideline for CYP2D6, OPRM1, and COMT genotypes and select opioid therapy.
references.relevance: Provides evidence-based recommendations to avoid codeine and tramadol in CYP2D6 PMs.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CYP2C9 PM status identified. [Gap] NSAID metabolism impaired, increasing toxicity risk. [Decision] To achieve safety profile improvement, chose to use CPIC Guidelines for CYP2C9 and NSAIDs to evaluate NSAID dosing, expecting to obtain recommendation for dose reduction or alternative.

tool.name: CPIC Guidelines for CYP2C9 and NSAIDs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recommendation to initiate with 25-50% dose or consider alternative therapy

references.citation: Theken KN, Lee CR, Gong L et al (2020) Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium guideline (CPIC) for CYP2C9 and nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs.
references.relevance: Links CYP2C9 genotype to phenotypic variability and adverse event risk for NSAIDs.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Neuropathic pain requires adjuvant treatment; CYP2D6/CYP2C19 status known. [Gap] TCA metabolism altered by genotype and drug interactions (bupropion). [Decision] To achieve effective neuropathy management, chose to use CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 and TCAs to evaluate amitriptyline suitability, expecting to obtain recommendation for alternative therapy.

tool.name: CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 and TCAs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recommendation to consider alternative adjuvant therapies

references.citation: Hicks JK, Sangkuhl K, Swen JJ et al (2017) Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium guideline (CPIC) for CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 genotypes and dosing of tricyclic antidepressants: 2016 update.
references.relevance: Recommends avoiding amitriptyline in CYP2C19 and/or CYP2D6 PMs or using lower doses with monitoring.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Standard options limited by PGx profile. [Gap] Need analgesic not dependent on CYP2D6 metabolism. [Decision] To achieve pain relief, chose to use CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and select opioid therapy to select hydromorphone, expecting to obtain significant pain relief.

tool.name: CPIC Guidelines for CYP2D6 and select opioid therapy

references.citation: Crews KR, Monte AA, Huddart R et al (2021) Clinical Pharmacogenetics Implementation Consortium guideline for CYP2D6, OPRM1, and COMT genotypes and select opioid therapy.
references.relevance: Identifies opioids like hydromorphone that are not metabolized by CYP2D6 as suitable alternatives.

references.citation: Owusu Obeng A, Hamadeh I, Smith M (2017) Review of opioid pharmacogenetics and considerations for pain management.
references.relevance: Confirms hydromorphone is not metabolized by the cytochrome system like codeine/tramadol.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Theoretical Case Study / Clinical Outcome Monitoring

pain_score_reduction.interpretation: Effective pain management achieved after PGx-guided adjustment

adverse_events.interpretation: Safety profile improved by avoiding metabolically incompatible drugs

03_success_verification.final_verdict: PGx integration aided in more precise applications of pain pharmacotherapy based on CPIC guidelines, achieving improved clinical response and safety profile.
